message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): premature server close returns Err (tick 345)

Added `distributed_http_client_surfaces_premature_server_close_as_err`
— pins that the client returns `Err` (not a truncated `Ok` and not a
hang) when the server begins writing a response but closes the
connection before the full `Content-Length` is delivered.

Test setup: server sends headers promising 100 bytes, writes 5 bytes of
body, then closes the connection. Client must return Err within 5s.

Why this matters: this is a realistic network interruption mode —
flaky connection, server OOM-killed mid-response, upstream restarted
during a long response. The client's response reader has three
possible behaviors under this scenario:

1. Return Err(_) — caller knows the response is incomplete and can
   retry, log, surface the failure to the operator. This is correct.
2. Return Ok(Response { body: <5 bytes> }) — leaks corrupt/partial
   data into the call site as if it were a successful response.
   Would cause downstream logic to deserialize truncated JSON, accept
   partial pane scrollback, etc.
3. Hang forever waiting for the remaining 95 bytes — stalls the
   containing agent workflow indefinitely.

Previously untested. A regression that changed the close-handling
path from option 1 to option 2 would silently corrupt distributed
event forwarding without any observable error. This test pins that
the client surfaces the truncation as an error.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 15s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
